competitive_position.summary: Contested RFP with the field still undisclosed: as of 19 Jun our pricing is 'in line with other vendors', so we are commercially competitive but not differentiated on price. The most concrete known alternative is the incumbent manual stack (Maximo + OnBase + SAP); Metallus will not leave Maximo, so we must win on integrating with and automating around it. The highest-leverage unknown is the identity of the other shortlisted vendors — Nicholas's live 29 Jun thread is the opening to ask before the 4 Jul down-select.

competitors.name: Unknown competitor(s)
competitors.quote: Nick said... pricing is in line with other vendors. They would evaluate vendor proposals over the next 2 weeks.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin AI/agentic differentiation (invoice exception handling, payment optimization, contract compliance automation) as the Phase 1 or Phase 2 centerpiece; position Zycus as the only vendor delivering next-gen AP intelligence, not just eInvoicing plumbing. Arm Nicholas to broker the budget-owner intro rather than cold-outreaching the EB. Post-shortlist, run competitive discovery to map the field and sharpen the wedge.

competitors.name: Undisclosed RFP competitors (multiple bidders confirmed)
competitors.quote: We have begun the process of reviewing all of our bidders proposals for this project and we are expecting to share feedback on who we will be pursuing soon.
competitors.how_we_win: Use Nicholas's live 29 Jun thread to ask who else is shortlisted and the criteria weighting; lead the finalist case with Merlin's negotiation agent, supplier-price-history intelligence and contract-anomaly detection plus the unified physical+service flow, not price.

competitors.name: Incumbent manual process (Maximo / OnBase / SAP, manual AP)
competitors.quote: The current invoice processing through Maximo is a manual and error-prone process, necessitating automation... for efficient three-way matching and exception handling.
competitors.how_we_win: Position as the AI three-way-match + exception engine that integrates with (not replaces) Maximo/SAP and replaces only OnBase approval routing; quantify error/rework cost of staying manual.

champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery 22 May 2026; demo 5 Jun 2026; Clari Email 29 Jun 2026; SF Next_Step__c
champion_strength.summary: Nicholas Galbincea is an active, engaged champion who drives the agenda, keeps cadence, and proactively emailed in on 29 Jun, but is manager-level and the sole decision-layer thread with no confirmed access to the budget owner.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is mapped. We are engaged at manager and director level: Nicholas Galbincea (Manager, Commodity & Supply Chain) and Stephen Murphy (Director, General Procurement) self-introduced on the 22 May call, with Robert Rummer (Sr. Manager, AR/AP & Credit) and Valerie Johnson attending. Pricing has been discussed and was 'in line with other vendors' (19 Jun), and on 29 Jun Nicholas himself asked the pricing-clarification question — but there is still no evidence of who owns the budget or signs the ~$293k year-one envelope, and no VP/C-level contact has appeared on any call. With the shortlist likely escalating to senior stakeholders we have not met, identifying and reaching the budget owner is the single biggest open structural risk. Because this is a champion-authored RFP, the right path is to arm Nicholas to broker the introduction — use his 29 Jun reply to ask him directly who signs off and request the connect.

recommended_moves: Reply to Nicholas's 29 Jun email confirming the implementation fee is on top of the $127,400 year-one SaaS fee, and in the same thread ask (a) who signs off on the spend and (b) who else is shortlisted.
Arm Nicholas to broker an introduction to the budget owner ahead of the shortlist decision.
Schedule the Professional Services scoping session and commit to the firm PS cost (with the possible reduction) owed ~15 Jul.
